text: An edge deployment scenario requires selecting integer quantities of 5 candidate models under shared resource limits. XGBoost consumes 0.76GB of RAM and 31 GFLOPS, delivering 0.96 accuracy at a latency of 66ms. Running CNN requires 0.42GB memory and 38 GFLOPS of compute; it reaches 0.85 accuracy with 11ms latency. SVM-linear needs 0.73GB RAM, 19 GFLOPS, and returns 0.88 accuracy in 18ms. KNN consumes 0.66GB of RAM and 18 GFLOPS, delivering 0.93 accuracy at a latency of 29ms. Running DecisionTree requires 0.43GB memory and 19 GFLOPS of compute; it reaches 0.91 accuracy with 61ms latency. The combined resource ceiling permits at most 3.7GB of memory, 92 GFLOPS of compute throughput, and 123ms of cumulative latency across everything deployed. To qualify as SOTA-capable, the deployment must include at least one instance of a model whose accuracy reaches 0.9 or higher; fractional deployments are not permitted. Work out how many of each model to deploy so the total weighted score (0.7 times accuracy plus 0.3 divided by latency, summed across every deployed unit) is as large as possible, and confirm every limit and the accuracy requirement are satisfied by your plan.

Running SVM-linear requires 1.43GB memory and 6 GFLOPS of compute; it reaches 0.91 accuracy with 44ms latency. CNN needs 0.83GB RAM, 32 GFLOPS, and returns 0.92 accuracy in 48ms. DecisionTree consumes 0.69GB of RAM and 39 GFLOPS, delivering 0.88 accuracy at a latency of 62ms. Running XGBoost requires 1.02GB memory and 6 GFLOPS of compute; it reaches 0.82 accuracy with 20ms latency. TinyBERT needs 1.21GB RAM, 17 GFLOPS, and returns 0.86 accuracy in 18ms. The combined resource ceiling permits at most 2.5GB of memory, 118 GFLOPS of compute throughput, and 99ms of cumulative latency across everything deployed. To qualify as SOTA-capable, the deployment must include at least one instance of a model whose accuracy reaches 0.9 or higher; fractional deployments are not permitted.
